title: Jayson Tatum earns his first Eastern Conference player of the week award of the season
author: Khari A. Thompson
bullets: Jayson Tatum wins Eastern Conference player of the week, his 13th career honor, after returning from Achille's tendon injury.
Tatum averaged 25.7 points, 9.7 rebounds, and 6.7 assists in three wins against top conference teams Thunder, Hawks, and Hornets.
Tatum becomes the youngest player in Celtics history to reach 14,000 career points, ranking third in franchise player of the week awards.

title: Celtics get good news on Jaylen Brown’s Achilles tendinitis injury
author: Conor Ryan
bullets: Jaylen Brown is cleared to play against the Atlanta Hawks after missing two games with Achilles tendinitis.
Derrick White is probable for Monday with a right knee contusion; Jayson Tatum is out for Achilles management.
Tatum earned NBA Eastern Conference Player of the Week, averaging 25.7 points, 9.7 rebounds, 6.7 assists over three games.

title: Major pharmaceutical company to cut nearly 250 Massachusetts jobs
author: Autumn Sloboda
bullets: Takeda Pharmaceutical to cut nearly 250 jobs in Cambridge as part of a $1.25 billion annual savings plan.
Layoffs at the 500 Kendall St. location will begin in July, extending into 2026 and 2027.
Other life sciences companies like Moderna and Thermo Fisher Scientific have also announced recent layoffs in Massachusetts.

title: Lexington to cut a slew of teachers as town prepares to build $660M high school
author: Abby Patkin
bullets: Lexington plans to cut 65 full-time equivalent positions due to budget issues while building a new $660M high school.
160 educators without professional teacher status in Lexington received non-renewal notices amid financial restructuring.
Superintendent Julie Hackett cites declining enrollment and rising costs as primary drivers for staffing cuts.

title: At 22, she’s running Boston for her mom — and a future without Alzheimer’s
author: Kelly Chan
bullets: Ella Wright, 22, is running the Boston Marathon to honor her mother and raise awareness for early-onset Alzheimer's.
Wright has lost several family members to Alzheimer's, including her grandfather, great uncle, and aunt.
Her mother was diagnosed in her forties, inspiring Wright to advocate for Alzheimer's research and caregiver support.

title: Plymouth officer charged with child rape suspended by POST Commission
author: Abby Patkin
bullets: Plymouth Police Officer Samantha Pelrine charged with child rape, arraigned alongside husband Daniel Forand in Plymouth District Court.
Peace Officer Standards and Training Commission suspended Pelrine's certification, requiring her to surrender all police equipment.
Pelrine and Forand, accused of abusing a boy between 2018-2025, posted bail; court hearing is set for June 8.

title: After 7 e-bike incidents in 10 days, Mass. police chief warns of impending ‘tragedy’
author: Samantha Genzer
bullets: Milford police warn of serious consequences after seven e-bike incidents in 10 days, including a crash involving a 15-year-old.
Massachusetts law allows riders over 16 to use e-bikes without a license, but helmets are mandatory for all.
Recent e-bike incidents in Massachusetts, including fatalities, have spurred debate over regulations and enforcement.

title: Bridgewater police warn of Tom Brady impersonation scam
author: Samantha Genzer
bullets: Bridgewater police issue warning about Tom Brady impersonation scam after a local family was targeted by a fraudster.
Victim sent gift cards to scammer pretending to be former Patriots quarterback, leading to police intervention.
FTC advises against sending money or gift cards to unknown individuals to prevent falling for imposter scams.

title: This Boston Marathon runner is supporting children in their hardest times
author: Kelly Chan
bullets: Maria Castaneda is running the 2026 Boston Marathon to support The Children's Room, a nonprofit aiding grieving children and families.
Castaneda, originally from Mexico and now residing in Cambridge, appreciates the Boston community's kindness and compassion.
She views her marathon participation as an opportunity to give back to an organization that supports children through challenging times.

title: Review and setlist: Lady Gaga’s Mayhem Ball brought the spectacle, but Boston brought the heart
author: Autumn Sloboda
bullets: Lady Gaga's concert at TD Garden in Boston featured her tour 'Mayhem Ball' with gothic theatrics and fan tributes.
During a piano performance, fans raised signs celebrating Gaga's 40th birthday, leaving her visibly emotional.
The concert included songs from Gaga's new album 'Mayhem' and older hits like 'Poker Face' and 'Born This Way'.
